The image presents a document titled "Government College University, Faisalabad Affiliation Rules." The document is divided into three sections: Definitions, Legal Formalities, and Application for Affiliation.

*   **Definitions**
    *   The section provides definitions for various terms used in the document, including:
        *   Ordinance
        *   University
        *   Affiliation
        *   Affiliation Committee
        *   Commission
        *   Higher Education Department
        *   Inspection committee
        *   College or Institute
        *   Prescribed
        *   Principal
        *   Syndicate
        *   Vice Chancellor
        *   Body

*   **Legal Formalities**
    *   This section outlines the legal requirements for affiliation, including:
        *   The head of the college/institute must produce a No Objection Certificate from the concerned Department of Government for starting new departments/subjects.
        *   Government Colleges/Institutes may seek permission from the Director (Colleges) concerned before applying to the University for affiliation.
        *   The ownership of a private institution must vest in a registered body, not in an individual(s) or family, registered under the relevant laws of Companies Ordinance/Societies Registration act / trust as a foundation / society / trust etc.
        *   No change in ownership of the body will be permissible after affiliation without prior approval of the University.
        *   No Educational Institution situated within the territorial limits of the University shall, without consent of the University and sanction of Higher Education Department for Provincial jurisdiction and, of Commission for Federal Capital Territory, be associated in any way with or seek affiliation with any other University.
        *   In case of a private sector College/Institute, a copy of the Registration Deed alongwith a Memorandum of Association will be supplied to examine the objectives and credentials of the members of the body.

*   **Application for Affiliation**
    *   This section outlines the procedure for applying for affiliation, including:
        *   A College or Institute applying for affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University on a form prescribed by the University within the date fixed by the University. In case of failure to apply for affiliation within due date, double or triple fee, as the case may be, shall be charged.
        *   In case of Government institutions, head of the institution will submit application through proper channel.
        *   In case of private institutions, it shall be submitted by the Principal or nominee of the management of the governing body.

In summary, the document outlines the rules and procedures for affiliation with Government College University, Faisalabad, including definitions, legal formalities, and the application process.
## Extracted Information

The provided image appears to be a section of a document outlining the regulations and requirements for colleges or institutes. The following structured information has been extracted:

### 4. Processing and Visiting Fee

* **Government College/Institute:**
	+ Application processing and visiting fee: Rs. 15,000
	+ Revisit fee: Rs. 10,000
* **Private Institution:**
	+ Application processing and visiting fee: Rs. 40,000
	+ Revisit fee: Rs. 20,000
* **Note:** 
	+ The Application Processing and Visiting Fee is non-refundable.
	+ The University may change/enhance Processing and Visiting Fee, subject to approval of its Statutory Bodies.

### 5. Location

1. **Building Requirements:**
	* The college/institute must be located in a spacious, separate, and independent building.
	* The building should be housed on an area of at least 4 kanals.
	* Alternatively, multi-story buildings covering 4000 sq ft area may be considered, along with other requisite requirements (e.g., Playground) in case of Private Institutions.
2. **No Objection Certificate:**
	* A No Objection Certificate from local bodies regarding suitability of the proposed location for establishment of the college/institute shall be provided.
3. **Site Requirements:**
	* The site selected for the college/institute must be suitable from an academic point of view.
	* Sustainable physical viability, availability of water, electricity, fuel gas, telephones must be ensured.
4. **Accessibility and Affiliation:**
	* The site existing or selected for the college/institute must be easily accessible to the general public.
	* The affiliation granted shall be restricted to a specified Place and for a particular subject.

### 6. Change in Premises

* No change in premises will be made without prior permission of the University.
* The new site will be visited by the Affiliation Committee before approval, subject to payment of prescribed Visit Fee.

### 7. Rented Building

* Where a college/institute is proposed to be located in rented premises, the management shall ensure that the institution will acquire its own building within a period of five to ten years, preferably in a non-residential area.

### 8. College/Institute in More than One Buildings

* An affiliated college or institute can be allowed to function in more than one building provided that administrative and academic control is centralized and the buildings are within reasonable and convenient distance from each other.

### 9. Hostel Facilities

* Adequate hostel facilities must be separately provided for admitted male and female students unless otherwise dispensed with.
## **Structured Information Extracted from the Page**

### **10 - Requirement of the Building**
1. **Classrooms and Common Rooms:**
   - Classrooms, common room, library, laboratories, and common room for girls.
   - Common room for girls: at least 16'x32' sq. ft. (not exceeding 40 students per section).

2. **Laboratory Size:**
   - Laboratory for Basic/Natural/Applied Science: at least 20'x40' sq. ft.

3. **Facilities:**
   - Auditorium, offices, classrooms, ladies' common room with attached toilet, and staff room.
   - Canteen, toilets, dispensary, indoor and outdoor games.

4. **Library and Laboratory Facilities:**
   - Well-stocked library and equipped laboratory facilities.
   - For IT fields: well-equipped IT laboratory with internet connectivity.

### **11 - Strength of the Students**
- **Maximum Students per Section:** 50 students.
- **Minimum Space per Student:** 12 sq. ft. per student.

### **12 - Examination Premises**
- The affiliated college/institute must provide premises for the University Examination equal to the number of admitted students.

### **13 - Principal, Teaching Staff, and Library Staff**
1. **Principal:**
   - Duly qualified whole-time employee.

2. **Teaching Staff:**
   - At least two M.Phil or one Ph.D. teacher for each BS Program.
   - 50% of teaching members in private sector colleges must be on permanent basis with M.Phil degree.

3. **Workload:**
   - Faculty workload in private colleges should not exceed University-prescribed limits.

4. **Sports Officer and Librarian:**
   - Whole-time qualified Sports Officer and Librarian with Master’s degree.

5. **Librarian:**
   - At least one Library Assistant and other essential staff.

6. **Change of Principal/Staff:**
   - Intimation to the University within 15 days.

### **14 - Library and Internet Facility**
1. **Library Space:**
   - Library should be located in a covered area for at least 20% of enrolled students.

2. **Initial Library Books:**
   - Worth at least Rs. 200,000.

3. **Library Collection:**
   - At least 50 reference books per subject.
   - At least 200 supplementary reading books per subject.

4. **Library Budget:**
   - Spend at least Rs. 100,000 per annum on new editions and titles.

5. **Subscriptions:**
   - 10 daily newspapers and 5 weekly periodicals.
**Structured Information Extracted from the Page**

### **Section 4: Internet Connectivity**

* The college/institute must have Internet connectivity with an appropriate number of computers depending on the student's enrollment and the subjects offered.

### **Section 15: Book Bank**

* The college/institute shall establish a Book Bank capable of providing prescribed text books to at least **10% of the students on roll**.

### **Section 16: Co-curricular & Health Care Facilities**

1. The college/institute shall promote extracurricular and recreational activities of students and make arrangements for promoting their health and general welfare.
2. Co-curricular activities include:
	* Debating contest
	* Declamation contest
	* Quran Khawani
	* Naat Khawani
	* Holding of Musharahas
	* Camp-Fire
	* Boy Scouts
	* Bids Guides activities
	* Excursions to historically important places and students' visits to:
		+ Factories
		+ Stock Exchanges
		+ Chambers of Commerce & industries
		+ Other educational trips
3. The college/institute should provide:
	* Indoor and outdoor facilities for games
	* Encourage students to participate in **one/two games**
	* Facilities of a gymnasium (if possible)
4. A part-time Medical Officer with medical equipment shall be available to meet emergency cases.

### **Section 17: Constitutional and Cultural Adherence**

1. The College/Institute shall frame proper rules regarding the efficiency and discipline of its staff and other employees.
2. The college/institute shall strictly comply with and respect:
	* Constitutional provisions
	* Local laws
	* Cultural and religious sensitivities
3. The college/institute must restrict activities to:
	* Teaching
	* Training
	* Co-curricular activities
	* (No political or other activities detrimental to national, religious, social, or local culture shall be undertaken)
4. Sponsors will provide:
	* Career counseling
	* Job search services to students

### **Section 18: Evening Classes**

1. The total number of students in evening classes shall not exceed the students admitted in the **morning classes**.
2. The College/Institute shall not be allowed to have **evening classes only**.

### **Section 19: Attendance Record**

* An authenticated attendance record of all students should be maintained and displayed on the Notice Board in the **first week of every succeeding month** in accumulative form.

### **Section 20: Maintenance of Record**

1. The College/Institute shall maintain records regarding:
	* Students' admission
	* Attendance
	* Fee concession
	* Appointment of staff
	* Their joining reports
	* Payment of salaries
	* Stock register
	* Accession register of library
2. The record shall not be removed from institution premises and remain available for inspection at all times by the **Affiliation Committee**.
### **Structured Information from the Page**

## **21. Payment of the Salaries**
- **1. Salary Structure**:  
  - Whole-time Faculty, Librarian, and Sports Officer must be paid according to the minimum scales prescribed by the University for their positions.  
- **2. Payment Method**:  
  - Salaries must be paid through crossed cheques or bank transfers to all employees.

## **22. Fee Concession**
- **Requirement**:  
  - At least **10%** of students should be granted fee concessions and scholarships on a need basis.

## **23. Violation of Rules**
- **1. Compliance with University Instructions**:  
  - The College/Institute must follow all instructions/ regulations issued by the University from time to time.  
- **2. Advertising Admissions**:  
  - Affiliated Colleges/Institutes may advertise admissions in print or digital media.  
- **3. Penalty for Violations**:  
  - Any violation of rules or deliberate misrepresentation of facts is grounds for withdrawing affiliation or imposing penalties.

## **24. Curricula**
- **Requirement**:  
  - The College/Institute must strictly follow the Curriculum/Syllabus/Schemes of Studies provided by the University for affiliated programs.

## **25. Examination and Evaluation**
- **1. University Oversight**:  
  - All examinations leading to degrees/diplomas/certificates and their evaluation are conducted by the University.  
- **2. Admission Restrictions**:  
  - The College/Institute cannot admit students for any degree/discipline except those awarded by the University.

## **26. Financial**
- **1. Financial Stability**:  
  - The College/Institute must be stable and capable of sustaining regular operations.  
- **2. Reporting Requirements**:  
  - The College/Institute must provide reports, returns, and other information as required by the University to assess financial sustainability.  
- **3. Endowment Fund**:  
  - Sponsors must establish an endowment fund of **Rs. 1 million** for laboratories, libraries, and educational gadgets.  
- **4. Working Capital**:  
  - A working capital of at least **Rs. 2.0 million** must be available for smooth operations.  
- **5. Tangible Assets**:  
  - Tangible assets (e.g., land, buildings) worth at least **Rs. 5.0 million** must exist on the ground.  

**Note**: Financial arrangements must be supported by documentary evidence.

## **27. Inspection and Affiliation Requirements**
- **1. Affiliation Period**:  
  - **Government Colleges/Institutes**: Initial affiliation for **3 years**.  
  - **Private Colleges/Institutes**: Initial affiliation for **1 year**.  
  - Affiliation may be extended based on performance.  
- **2. Extension Process**:  
  - The College/Institute management must apply for affiliation extension on the prescribed form with required documents and fees.
## **Structured Information Extracted from the Document**

### **Section 2-12: Affiliation Terms and Conditions**

#### **2. Validity Period of Agreements**
- **Validity Period**: The validity period of agreements must be clearly agreed upon by all parties.
- **Extension Provision**: Any extension of the agreement must specify the requirements for review.

#### **3. Affiliation Date**
- **No Retrospective Affiliation**: No affiliation will be granted with a retrospective session.

#### **4. University’s Discretion on Affiliation**
- **Discretionary Powers**: The University has the discretion to:
  - Grant
  - Suspend
  - Cancel
  affiliation in any subject.
- **Appeal Rights**: The affected party has the right to a one-time appeal to the University authority according to the rules.

#### **5. Affiliation Committee and Inspection**
- **Constitution of Affiliation Committee**: The University has the power to constitute an Affiliation Committee.
- **Inspection Rights**: The University can arrange inspections at any time and conduct surprise visits.

#### **6. Verification of College/Institute Infrastructure**
- **Pre-Affiliation Survey**: The Affiliation Committee must conduct a detailed survey of the College/Institute before granting affiliation.
- **Subject-Wise Affiliation**: Affiliation will be granted subject-wise.
- **Information Requirements**: The College/Institute must provide reports, returns, and other required information to assess efficiency and effectiveness.

#### **7. Monitoring and Inspection**
- **Monitoring by Affiliation Committee**: Monitoring and inspection will be carried out by the Affiliation Committee if there is substantial evidence of inefficiency or malfunctioning.

#### **8. University’s Action on Colleges/Institutes**
- **Authority to Take Action**: The University may require Colleges/Institutes to take necessary actions as deemed fit under the law.

#### **9. University’s Powers**
- **Discretionary Actions**: The University has full powers to take appropriate actions, including:
  - Disaffiliation
  - Closure
  if the College/Institute engages in:
  - Subversive activities
  - Unlawful activities
  - Violations of any law

#### **10. Provision of Facilities**
- **Liability**: The College/Institute must provide facilities to representatives of:
  - Higher Education Commission
  - Punjab Higher Education Department
  - The University
  for visits to verify academic standards.

#### **11. Annual Financial Statement**
- **Annual Statement**: The College/Institute must furnish an audited statement of:
  - Fees
  - Donations
  - Other income
  - Expenditure
  within two months of the end of each financial year.

#### **12. Inspection Schedule**
- **Inspection Frequency**:
  - **First Three Years**: Inspection once a year by the Inspection Committee.
  - **Subsequent Years**: Inspection once every three years.

### **Section 28: Formal Agreement for Affiliation**

#### **1. Formal Affiliation Arrangements**
- **Formal Notification**: All affiliation arrangements must be:
  - Agreed upon
  - Formally notified
  by the University.
- **Detailed Terms**: The notification must include detailed terms and conditions of affiliation.

#### **2. Notification Contents**
- **Scope of Arrangements**: The notification must cover:
  - Scope of arrangements
  - Responsibilities
  - Financial arrangements
  - Quality control mechanisms
  - Mode and means of payments
  - Validity period
  - Procedure for resolving differences

#### **3. Quality Control Mechanism**
- **Provisions**: The notification must include provisions for:
  - Quality control mechanisms
  - Monitoring
  - Assessment procedures
  - Review and visitation

#### **4. Termination of Affiliation**
- **Student Interests**: The notification must ensure that in the event of termination of affiliation, student interests are safeguarded and notified to:
  - The general public
  - The Commission

### **Section 29: University Dues/Share**

#### **a. Security Fee**
- **Government Colleges/Institutes**: No security fee will be charged.
- **Head of College/Institute Responsibility**: The head must provide assurance as specified.

### **Structured Information Extracted from the Page**

## **1. Fee Structure**
### **Semester System Programs**
| **Institution Type**         | **Fee Structure**                          |
|-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| **Government Colleges/Institutes** | Rs. 3,000 + 30 = Rs. 3,030/- per semester (non-refundable) |
| **Private Colleges/Institutes**   | Rs. 5,000 + 30 = Rs. 5,030/- per semester (non-refundable) |

### **Annual System Programs**
| **Program Type**               | **Fee Structure**                     |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| ADA/ADC (non-practical subjects) | Rs. 2,500/- per annum (non-refundable) |
| ADA (practical subjects)        | Rs. 3,000/- per annum (non-refundable) |
| ADS and other subjects         | Rs. 3,000/- per annum (non-refundable) |

### **Note**
- The University reserves the right to change/enhance any of the mentioned fees, subject to approval by its Statutory Bodies.



## **2. Constitution of Affiliation Committee**
### **Composition of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Chairperson**: Nominated by the Syndicate.
2. **Two Professors**: Nominated by the Academic Council.
3. **Two Teachers**: Nominated by the Syndicate.
4. **Deputy Director (Colleges) / Senior Principal**: Nominated by the Syndicate from any Government College of District Faisalabad.

### **Term of Office**
- The term of office for members (excluding ex-officio members) is **two years**.

### **Co-option of Experts**
- The Affiliation Committee may co-opt up to **three experts** for inspections/visits, who count towards the quorum.

### **Quorum**
- The quorum for meetings or inspections/visits is **three members**.

### **Secretary of the Committee**
- The **Deputy Registrar** (or a person designated by the Vice Chancellor) acts as the Secretary.



## **3. Functions of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Inspect Educational Institutions**: 
   - Inspect institutions seeking affiliation or admission to the privileges of the University.
   - Advise the Syndicate.

2. **Investigate Complaints**: 
   - Look into complaints about breaches of affiliation conditions by affiliated colleges.
   - Advise the Syndicate.

3. **Other Functions**: 
   - Perform additional functions as prescribed by the Regulations.



## **4. Teaching Staff and Other Conditions**
### **Undergraduate Colleges (Offering Associate Degree Programs Only)**
- **Principal and Teaching Staff**: Must have at least a **Master’s degree** in the relevant subject.
- **Principal’s Experience**: Five years of teaching/administrative experience at a reputed institution.
- **Laboratory Requirement**: 
  - A **separate, independent, well-equipped laboratory** must be established for each subject of experimental science.

### **Postgraduate Colleges**
- **Minimum Teaching Staff**: At least **seven teachers**.
- **Qualification Requirements**: 
  - At least **one Ph.D. or two M.Phil qualified permanent teachers** for each subject.
- **Laboratory Requirement**: 
  - A **separate, independent, well-equipped laboratory** for each subject of experimental science.
- **Library Requirement**: 
  - At least **five hundred title books** must be available in the Post-Graduate Library.
The image presents a document outlining the requirements for various types of colleges in Pakistan. The document is divided into sections, each addressing specific types of colleges and their respective requirements.

*   **Commerce / Management Science Colleges**
    *   There shall be minimum six permanent teachers, out of which, there must be one Ph.D or at least two M.Phil teachers in each program.
    *   There shall be a Computer Laboratory properly equipped to accommodate 20 percent of the admitted students at a time.
    *   At least five hundred title books should be available in the Library.
*   **Law Colleges**
    *   Principal of a law college should be full-time employee of the college having LLM degree with at least ten year teaching experience from a reputed institution.
    *   There shall be at least six whole-time teachers with LLM qualification.
    *   At least one Ph.D or two LLM qualified permanent teachers for LLB.
    *   The minimum required number of library books is 500, excluding reports and journals.
    *   The Library of Law College should subscribe at least two reputable international law journals or have access to law journals through internet.
    *   Furthermore, there should be at least one set of all law reports published in Pakistan, till date.
*   **Pharmacy Colleges**
    *   There shall be minimum 8 teachers for B Pharmacy and 12 teachers for Pharm D classes in the following disciplines:
        1.  Pharmaceutics
        2.  Pharmaceutical Chemistry
        3.  Pharmacognosy
        4.  Pharmacology
    *   There shall be at least one PhD or two M.Phil teachers in each discipline.
    *   Principal of the college shall be full-time employee having PhD degree in any of the above mentioned disciplines with at least ten year teaching / research experience in relevant field from a reputed institution.
    *   For each discipline, a separate / independent well-equipped laboratory shall be established.
    *   At least five hundred title books should be available in the Library.

The information on this document can be organized into the following categories:

**1. Definitions**

* **Ordinance**: Government College University, Faisalabad Ordinance, 2002
* **University**: Government College University, Faisalabad
* **Affiliation**: affiliation with the University
* **Affiliation Committee**: the Affiliation Committee constituted under Section 10 of the First Schedule appended to the Ordinance
* **Commission**: Higher Education Commission
* **Higher Education Department**: Punjab Higher Education Department
* **Inspection committee**: the committee executing inspection of the institute
* **College or Institute**: the college or institute seeking affiliation with the University
* **Prescribed**: prescribed by the University
* **Principal**: the head of the college or the chief executive of the institute applying for affiliation
* **Syndicate**: the Syndicate of the University
* **Vice Chancellor**: Vice Chancellor of the University
* **Body**: a Body registered under the relevant Act/Law etc.

**2. Legal Formalities**

* The head of the college/institute shall, at the time of applying for affiliation, produce No Objection Certificate from the concerned Department of Government for starting new departments/subjects.
* The Government Colleges/Institutes may also seek permission from the Director (Colleges) concerned before applying to the University for affiliation.
* The ownership of a private institution shall vest in a registered body and not in individual(s) or family, registered under the relevant laws of Companies Ordinance/Societies Registration act / trust as a foundation / society / trust etc. This shall not be required in case the college or institute is in the Public Sector.
* No change in ownership of the body will be permissible after affiliation without prior approval of the University.
* No Educational Institution situated within the territorial limits of the University shall, without consent of the University and sanction of Higher Education Department for Provincial jurisdiction and, of Commission for Federal Capital Territory, be associated in any way with or seek affiliation with any other University.
* In case of a private sector College/Institute, a copy of the Registration Deed alongwith a Memorandum of Association will be supplied to examine the objectives and credentials of the members of the body.

**3. Application for Affiliation**

* A College or Institute applying for affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University on a form prescribed by the University within the date fixed by the University. 
    * In case of failure to apply for affiliation within due date, double or triple fee, as the case may be, shall be charged.
* In case of Government institutions, head of the institution will submit application through proper channel. 
* In case of private institutions, it shall be submitted by the Principal or nominee of the management of the governing body.

* **Commission**: Higher Education Commission

* **Inspection committee**: committee executing inspection of the institute

* **Prescribed**: prescribed by the University

* **Vice Chancellor**: Vice Chancellor of the University
* **Body**: Body registered under the relevant Act/Law etc.

**2- Legal Formalities**

* The head of the college/institute shall produce No Objection Certificate from the concerned Department of Government for starting new departments/subjects.
* Government Colleges/Institutes may seek permission from the Director (Colleges) concerned before applying to the University for affiliation.
* Ownership of a private institution shall vest in a registered body and not in individual(s) or family, registered under the relevant laws of Companies Ordinance/Societies Registration act / trust as a foundation / society / trust etc.
* No change in ownership of the body will be permissible after affiliation without prior approval of the University.
* No Educational Institution situated within the territorial limits of the University shall, without consent of the University and sanction of Higher Education Department for Provincial jurisdiction and, of Commission for Federal Capital Territory, be associated in any way with or seek affiliation with any other University.
* In case of a private sector College/Institute, a copy of the Registration Deed alongwith a Memorandum of Association will be supplied to examine the objectives and credentials of the members of the body.

**3- Application for Affiliation**

* A College or Institute applying for affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University on a form prescribed by the University within the date fixed by the University. 
* In case of failure to apply for affiliation within due date, double or triple fee, as the case may be, shall be charged.
* In case of Government institutions, head of the institution will submit application through proper channel.
* In case of private institutions, it shall be submitted by the Principal or nominee of the management of the governing body.

## **2. Constitution of Affiliation Committee**
### **Composition of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Chairperson**: Nominated by the Syndicate.
2. **Professors**: Two professors nominated by the Academic Council.
3. **Teachers**: Two teachers nominated by the Syndicate.
4. **Deputy Director (Colleges) / Senior Principal**: Nominated by the Syndicate from a Government College in Faisalabad.

### **Term of Office**
- The term of office for members (excluding ex-officio members) is **two years**.

### **Co-option of Experts**
- The Committee may co-opt up to **three experts** who count towards the quorum for inspections/visits.

### **Quorum**
- The quorum for meetings or inspections/visits is **three members**.

### **Secretary of the Committee**
- The **Deputy Registrar** (or another person designated by the Vice Chancellor) acts as the Secretary.

## **3. Functions of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Inspect Educational Institutions**:  
   - Inspect institutions seeking affiliation or admission to the University's privileges.  
   - Advise the Syndicate on these matters.

2. **Handle Complaints**:  
   - Investigate complaints about breaches of affiliation conditions by affiliated colleges.  
   - Advise the Syndicate accordingly.

3. **Additional Functions**:  
   - Perform other functions as prescribed by the Regulations.

## **4. Teaching Staff and Other Conditions**
### **Undergraduate Colleges (Offering Associate Degree Programs Only)**
- **Principal and Teaching Staff**:  
  - Principal and teaching staff must have at least a **Master's degree** in the relevant subject.  
  - The principal should have **five years of teaching/administrative experience** at a reputable institution.

- **Laboratory Requirement**:  
  - A **separate, independent, well-equipped laboratory** must be established for each subject of experimental science.

### **Postgraduate Colleges**
- **Minimum Teaching Staff**: At least **seven teachers**.  
- **Qualification Requirements**:  
  - At least **one Ph.D. or two M.Phil qualified permanent teachers** per subject.  

- **Laboratory and Library Requirements**:  
  - A **separate, independent, well-equipped laboratory** for each experimental science subject.  
  - The **Post-Graduate Library** must have at least **five hundred title books**.

- **Laboratories**:
  - Separate, well-equipped laboratories for each discipline.

- **Library**:
  - At least 500 title books available.

- **Affiliation**:
  - Subject to approval by the Pharmacy Council of Pakistan.

**2-Legal Formalities:**

*   The head of the college/institute shall, at the time of applying for affiliation, produce No Objection Certificate from the concerned Department of Government for starting new departments/subjects.
*   The Government Colleges/Institutes may also seek permission from the Director (Colleges) concerned before applying to the University for affiliation.
*   The ownership of a private institution shall vest in a registered body and not in individual(s) or family, registered under the relevant laws of Companies Ordinance/Societies Registration act / trust as a foundation / society / trust etc. This shall not be required in case the college or institute is in the Public Sector.
*   No change in ownership of the body will be permissible after affiliation without prior approval of the University.
*   No Educational Institution situated within the territorial limits of the University shall, without consent of the University and sanction of Higher Education Department for Provincial jurisdiction and, of Commission for Federal Capital Territory, be associated in any way with or seek affiliation with any other University.
*   In case of a private sector College/Institute, a copy of the Registration Deed alongwith a Memorandum of Association will be supplied to examine the objectives and credentials of the members of the body.

**3-Application for Affiliation:**

*   A College or Institute applying for affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University on a form prescribed by the University within the date fixed by the University. In case of failure to apply for affiliation within due date, double or triple fee, as the case may be, shall be charged.
*   In case of Government institutions, head of the institution will submit application through proper channel.
*   In case of private institutions, it shall be submitted by the Principal or nominee of the management of the governing body.

- **Internet Connectivity**:  
  - The college/institute must have Internet connectivity with an appropriate number of computers depending on the student’s enrollment and the subjects offered.

- **Objective**:  
  - The college/institute shall establish a Book Bank capable of providing prescribed textbooks to at least **10% of the students on roll**.

## **Section 16: Co-curricular & Health Care Facilities**
### **Co-curricular Activities**:
1. Promote **extracurricular and recreational activities** for students and ensure their health and general welfare.
2. Provide activities such as:
   - Debating contests
   - Declamation contests
   - Quran Khawani
   - Naat Khawani
   - Musharahas
   - Campfires
   - Boy Scouts
   - Bids Guides activities
3. Organize **excursions** to:
   - Historically important places
   - Factories
   - Stock Exchanges
   - Chambers of Commerce & Industries
4. **Indoor and Outdoor Facilities**:
   - Ensure facilities for games to foster a sense of competition and tolerance.
   - Encourage students to participate in **at least one or two games**.
5. **Health Care**:
   - Engage a **part-time Medical Officer** with medical equipment for emergency cases.
   - Provide **gymnasium facilities** if possible.

## **Section 17: Constitutional and Cultural Adherence**
1. **Staff Discipline**:  
   - Frame proper rules regarding the efficiency and discipline of staff and other employees.
2. **Legal and Cultural Compliance**:  
   - Comply with **constitutional provisions**, **local laws**, and respect **cultural and religious sensitivities**.
3. **Restricted Activities**:  
   - Limit activities to **teaching, training, and co-curricular activities**.
   - Avoid **political or other activities** detrimental to national, religious, social, or local culture.
4. **Student Support**:  
   - Provide **career counseling and job search services** to students.

## **Section 18: Evening Classes**
1. **Student Enrollment Limit**:  
   - The total number of students in evening classes shall not exceed the number admitted in **morning classes**.
2. **Operational Restrictions**:  
   - The college/institute shall not be allowed to have **evening classes only**.

## **Section 19: Attendance Record**
- Maintain an **authenticated attendance record** of all students.
- Display the record on the **Notice Board** in the **first week of every succeeding month** in cumulative form.

## **Section 20: Maintenance of Record**
1. **Required Records**:  
   - Maintain records regarding:
     - Students’ **admission**
     - **Attendance**
     - **Fee concession**
     - Appointment of **staff**
     - **Salaries**
     - **Stock register**
     - **Accession register of the library**
2. **Record Accessibility**:  
   - Records shall not be removed from institution premises.
   - Records must be available for inspection at all times by the **Affiliation Committee**.

### **21 - Payment of the Salaries**
- **Whole time Faculty, Librarian, and Sports Officer**: 
  - Shall be paid the minimum scales prescribed by the University for corresponding positions.
- **Salaries Payment Method**:
  - Salaries shall be paid through crossed Cheques/Bank transfers to all employees.

### **22 - Fee Concession**
- **Fee Concession Policy**:
  - At least 10% of students should be granted fee concession and scholarships on a need basis.

### **23 - Violation of Rules**
- **Compliance with University Instructions**:
  1. The College / Institute shall follow all instructions / regulations issued by the University, from time to time.
- **Advertisement of Admissions**:
  2. Any affiliated College / Institute may advertise admissions in the print or other media.
- **Violation Consequences**:
  3. Any violation of the rules or deliberate misrepresentation of facts shall be treated as sufficient basis for the withdrawal of affiliation or to impose any penalty.

### **24 - Curricula**
- **Curriculum Compliance**:
  - The College/Institute shall strictly follow the Curriculum/Syllabus/Schemes of Studies provided by the University for Affiliated Programs.

### **25 - Examination and Evaluation**
- **Examination Authority**:
  1. All examinations leading to the award of degrees / diplomas / certificates and their checking and evaluation shall be done by the University.
- **Admissions for Degrees/Disciplines**:
  2. The College / Institute shall not admit students for any degree / discipline except those awarded by the University.

### **26 - Financial**
- **University Satisfaction**:
  1. Subject to satisfaction of the University, the College / Institute must be stable and must have the ability to sustain regular functioning and efficient working.
- **Financial Reports**:
  2. The College / Institute shall furnish such reports, returns, and other information as the University may require, to enable it to judge the financial sustainability and soundness of the sponsors.
- **Endowment Fund**:
  3. The sponsors shall, prior to enrolling students, establish an endowment fund of Rupees one million, whose interests be utilized for refurbishing the laboratories and libraries and for the provision of gadgets aimed at enhancing the quality of education.
- **Working Capital**:
  4. Working capital of at least Rs. 2.0 million must lie with the College / Institute for smooth functioning of their affairs.
- **Tangible Assets**:
  5. Tangible assets in the form of land, building, etc. not less than Rs. 5.0 million must be in existence on the ground.
- **Note**:
  - Above financial arrangements need to be supported by documentary evidence.

### **27 - Inspection and Affiliation Requirements**
- **Affiliation Duration**:
  1. Affiliation shall be initially for three years for Government Colleges / Institutes and for one year for private Colleges / Institutes, respectively. It may be extended from time to time depending upon the performance as ascertained through inspection.
- **Extension of Affiliation**:
  - The College / Institute management must have to apply for a grant of extension in affiliation on the prescribed form along with the prescribed documents and processing/visit fee.
## **Structured Information Extracted from the Document**

### **1. **Validity and Affiliation Period**  
2. The validity period of agreements must be agreed upon by all parties. Extensions require specifying review requirements.  
3. No retrospective session affiliations are allowed.  

### **2. **University Discretion and Appeal**  
4. The University can grant, suspend, or cancel affiliations in any subject. Affected parties have a one-time appeal right to the University authority.  

### **3. **Affiliation Committee and Inspection**  
5. The University can constitute an Affiliation Committee and arrange inspections at any time, including surprise visits.  
6. The Committee conducts a detailed survey before granting affiliation, which is subject-wise.  
7. Monitoring and inspection occur when there's evidence of institutional inefficiency or malfunctioning.  

### **4. **University Powers and Actions**  
8. The University can demand action from Colleges/Institutes as needed.  
9. The University can take actions, including disaffiliation, for subversive or unlawful activities.  

### **5. **College/Institute Responsibilities**  
10. Colleges/Institutes must provide facilities for visits from the Higher Education Commission, Punjab Higher Education Department, and the University.  
11. An annual audited statement of fees, donations, and income/expenditure must be submitted within two months of the financial year-end.  
12. Inspections occur:  
   - Once a year during the first three years of affiliation.  
   - Once every three years thereafter.  

### **6. **Formal Agreement for Affiliation**  
1. All affiliation arrangements must be formally notified by the University with detailed terms and conditions.  
2. The notification includes:  
   - Scope of arrangements  
   - Responsibilities  
   - Financial arrangements  
   - Quality control mechanisms  
   - Payment methods  
   - Validity period  
   - Dispute resolution  

3. The notification must detail quality control mechanisms, monitoring, assessment procedures, and visitation.  
4. Termination of affiliation requires safeguarding student interests and public notification.  

### **7. **University Dues/Share**  
#### **a. Security Fee**  
1. **Government Colleges/Institutes**: No security fee is charged. However, the head must provide assurances as required.

*   The ownership of a private institution shall vest in a registered body and not in individual(s) or family, registered under the relevant laws of Companies Ordinance/Societies Registration act / trust as a foundation / society / trust etc. This shall not be required in case the college or institute is in the Public Sector.
*   No change in ownership of the body will be permissible after affiliation without prior approval of the University.
*   No Educational Institution situated within the territorial limits of the University shall, without consent of the University and sanction of Higher Education Department for Provincial jurisdiction and, of Commission for Federal Capital Territory, be associated in any way with or seek affiliation with any other University.
*   In case of a private sector College/Institute, a copy of the Registration Deed alongwith a Memorandum of Association will be supplied to examine the objectives and credentials of the members of the body.

**3-Application for Affiliation**

1.  A College or Institute applying for affiliation to the University shall make an application to the University on a form prescribed by the University within the date fixed by the University. In case of failure to apply for affiliation within due date, double or triple fee, as the case may be, shall be charged.
2.  In case of Government institutions, head of the institution will submit application through proper channel.
3.  In case of private institutions, it shall be submitted by the Principal or nominee of the management of the governing body.

### 5. Location

*   **Requirements:**
    1.  The college/institute must be located in a spacious, separate, and independent building.
    2.  The building should be housed on an area of at least **4 kanals** (or **4000 sq ft** for multi-story buildings).
    3.  The site should have sustainable physical viability, including:
        *   Water
        *   Electricity
        *   Fuel gas
        *   Telephones
    4.  The site should be easily accessible to the general public.
*   **Additional Requirements for Private Institutions:**
    *   A **No Objection Certificate** from local bodies regarding the suitability of the proposed location is required.

### 6. Change in Premises

*   No change in premises will be made without prior permission from the University.
*   The new site will be visited by the Affiliation Committee before approval, subject to payment of the prescribed **Visit Fee**.

### 7. Rented Building

*   If a college/institute is proposed to be located in rented premises, the management must ensure that the institution will acquire its own building within **5 to 10 years**, preferably in a **non-residential area**.

### 8. College/Institute in More than One Building

*   An affiliated college or institute can function in more than one building if:
    *   Administrative and academic control is centralized.
    *   The buildings are within a reasonable and convenient distance from each other.

### 9. Hostel Facilities

*   Adequate hostel facilities must be separately provided for admitted male and female students unless otherwise dispensed with.

    *   The College/Institute shall maintain record regarding students' admission, attendance, fee concession, appointment of staff, their joining reports, payment of salaries, stock register and accession register of library.

- **Requirement**: At least **10% of students** should be granted **fee concession and scholarships** on a need basis.

## **23. Violation of Rules**
1. **Compliance**:

2. **Advertisement**:
   - Affiliated Colleges/Institutes may advertise admissions in **print or other media**.

3. **Penalty for Violations**:
   - Any violation of the rules or deliberate misrepresentation of facts shall be treated as a **sufficient basis for the withdrawal of affiliation** or to impose any penalty.

## **24. Curricula**
- **Compliance Requirement**:
  - The College/Institute shall **strictly follow the Curriculum/Syllabus/Schemes of Studies** provided by the University for Affiliated Programs.

## **25. Examination and Evaluation**
1. **Examination Authority**:
   - **All examinations** leading to the award of degrees/diplomas/certificates and their checking and evaluation shall be done by the **University**.

2. **Admissions**:
   - The College/Institute shall not admit students for any degree/discipline except those awarded by the **University**.

## **26. Financial**
1. **Financial Stability**:
   - The College/Institute must be **stable and able to sustain regular functioning** and efficient working, subject to the satisfaction of the University.

2. **Reporting Requirements**:
   - The College/Institute shall furnish **such reports, returns, and other information** as the University may require to judge the financial sustainability and soundness of the sponsors.

3. **Endowment Fund**:
   - Sponsors shall establish an **endowment fund of Rupees one million**, whose interests be utilized for:
     - Refurbishing the laboratories and libraries.
     - Provision of gadgets aimed at enhancing the quality of education.

4. **Working Capital**:
   - **At least Rs. 2.0 million** must lie with the College/Institute for smooth functioning.

5. **Tangible Assets**:
   - **Not less than Rs. 5.0 million** in the form of land, building, etc., must be in existence on the ground.

   - Affiliation may be extended from time to time depending upon performance as ascertained through **inspection**.

3. **Application for Extension**:
   - The College/Institute management must apply for the grant of extension in affiliation on the **prescribed form** along with the prescribed documents and **processing/visit fee**.

### **2. Constitution of Affiliation Committee**
#### **Members of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Chairperson**: Nominated by the Syndicate.
2. **Professors**: Two professors nominated by the **Academic Council**.
3. **Teachers**: Two teachers nominated by the **Syndicate**.
4. **Deputy Director (Colleges) / Senior Principal**: Nominated by the **Syndicate**.

#### **Term of Office**
- The term of office for members (excluding ex-officio members) is **two years**.

#### **Co-option of Experts**
- The Affiliation Committee may co-opt up to **three experts** for inspections/visits, who count towards the quorum.

#### **Quorum**
- The quorum for meetings or inspections/visits is **three members**.

#### **Secretary of the Committee**
- The **Deputy Registrar** or a person designated by the **Vice Chancellor** acts as Secretary.

### **3. Functions of the Affiliation Committee**
1. **Inspect Educational Institutions**: Inspect institutions seeking affiliation or admission to university privileges and advise the **Syndicate**.
2. **Investigate Complaints**: Handle complaints about breaches of affiliation conditions and advise the **Syndicate**.
3. **Additional Functions**: Perform other functions as prescribed by the **Regulations**.

### **4. Teaching Staff and Other Conditions**
#### **Undergraduate Colleges (Associate Degree Programs Only)**
- **Principal and Teaching Staff**: Must have at least a **Master’s degree** in the relevant subject.
- **Principal’s Experience**: Five years of teaching/administrative experience at a reputed institution.
- **Laboratory Requirement**: A separate, well-equipped laboratory for each **experimental science subject**.

#### **Postgraduate Colleges**
- **Minimum Teachers**: At least **seven teachers**.
- **Qualifications**: At least **one Ph.D. or two M.Phil qualified permanent teachers** per subject.
- **Laboratory Requirement**: Separate, well-equipped laboratories for each **experimental science subject**.
- **Library**: At least **500 title books** in the Post-Graduate Library.

    *   Merit: 70% weightage terminal degree and 30% weightage to test + Interview.
    *   Terminal Degrees:
        *   MS Agriculture Extension
        *   MPhil Agriculture Extension
        *   MSc (Hons) Agriculture Extension
        *   Or with any major in Agriculture

### **Programs and Admission Criteria**

1. **4-year BS Chemistry**
   - **Eligibility**: At least 45% marks in HSSC (Part-I/II) Pre-Medical / Pre-Engineering or Equivalent with Chemistry as an elective subject.

2. **4-year BS Biochemistry**
   - **Eligibility**: At least 45% marks in HSSC (Part-I/II) Pre-Medical or Equivalent qualification with Chemistry as an elective subject.
   - **Merit**: SSC-40%+ HSSC (Part-I / II)- 60% + 20 marks of Hafiz-e-Quran (if applicable)

3. **2-year BS Chemistry (5th Semester Intake)**
   - **Eligibility**:
     1. Minimum 25% or higher marks contribution of Chemistry subject to total (e.g., 200/800) is compulsory (with at least 50% marks) for graduates with biannual / annual examination system.
     2. Minimum 25% or higher credit hours (CRs) contribution of Chemistry subjects to total CRs (e.g., 15 out of 60 CRs) is compulsory (with at least 50% marks in each course) for graduates with semester system examination.
   - **Merit**: 8 × (%age Marks in annual / semester system) + 2 × [%marks obtained in Chemistry subject(s)] + 20 marks for Hafiz-e-Quran (if applicable)

4. **2-year MPhil Chemistry**
   - **Specializations**: 
     - Analytical / Inorganic Chemistry
     - Biochemistry
     - Organic Chemistry
     - Physical Chemistry
   - **Eligibility**: Entry Test conducted by Institute of Chemistry, UOS (Min. 50%) + Interview and 2.0 CGPA in BS/MSc chemistry/equivalent.
     - A candidate can only choose such program in which (s)he has specialized in the last year of study.
   - **Merit**: 70% weightage to terminal degree and 30% weightage to entry test + Interview.

### **Terminal Degrees**

   - FSc. Pre Medical
   - FSc. Pre Engineering

   - BSc.
   - ADP (biannual/annual) system
   - ADS (Semester system)

4. **2-year MPhil Chemistry**
   - **i. BS Chemistry (Specialization in Analytical/ Inorganic Chemistry) OR MSc Chemistry (Specialization in Analytical/ Inorganic Chemistry)**
   - **ii. BS Chemistry (Specialization in Biochemistry) OR MSc Chemistry (Specialization in Biochemistry)**
   - **iii. BS Chemistry (Specialization in Organic Chemistry)**

## **UNDERGRADUATE SCHOLARSHIPS AT LAHORE SCHOOL**

### **Need Based Scholarship**  
- **Number of Scholarships**: Limited number of 50% Need Based Scholarships available.  
- **Value**: 50% of total tuition fees for the term.  
- **Eligibility Criteria**:  
  - Intermediate Board with 75% marks and IBCC equivalence of 75% in ‘O’ and ‘A’ levels.  
  - Demonstrated financial need through:  
    1. Completed Financial Assistance Form.  
    2. Home verification visit by Financial Assistance Office staff.  
- **CGPA Requirement**:  
  - Maintain a CGPA of 3.5 to keep the scholarship.  
  - CGPA 3.49-3.0: Reduced to 25%.  
  - CGPA < 3.0: Not eligible.  

### **Special Need Based Scholarship**  
- **Eligibility**:  
  1. Single-parent students (father deceased or parents divorced) → 25% additional scholarship.  
  2. Students with disabilities meeting academic and financial criteria → up to 100% tuition fee waiver.  
- **CGPA Requirement**:  
  - Single-parent: Maintain CGPA 3.0.  
  - Disabilities: Maintain CGPA 2.5.  

### **Undergraduate Major Merit Scholarship**  
- **Eligibility**:  
  - Intermediate Board: 85% marks.  
  - IBCC equivalence: 85% or 3 A’s in ‘A’ Levels.  
- **Value**: 50% of total tuition fees for the term (first term, contingent on CGPA).  
- **CGPA Requirements**:  
  - Maintain CGPA 3.5 for 50% scholarship.  
  - CGPA 3.74-3.5: Reduced to 25%.  
  - CGPA < 3.5: Scholarship withdrawn.  

### **Undergraduate Minor Merit Scholarship**  
- **Eligibility**:  
  - **Year 1**: GPA 3.50 at end of first term → 2 credit hour course waiver in spring/summer.  
  - **Year 2-4**: GPA 4.0 → 4 credit hour course waiver.  
- **Exclusions**: Not with Major Merit or Need Based Scholarships.  

### **Additional Information**  
- **Deadline**: July 15th of the relevant admission academic year for Financial Assistance.  
- **Exclusivity**:  
  - Major Merit Scholarship and Need Based Scholarship are mutually exclusive.  
  - Minor Merit Scholarships do not combine with Major Merit or Need Based Scholarships.
### **Structured Information from the Page**

## **1. Merit Scholarship (MMS II)**
- **Eligibility**:  
  - In addition to Major Merit Scholarship and Need Based Scholarships.
  - No specific details on eligibility criteria.

## **2. Student TA Ships**
- **Eligibility**:  
  - Students in 2nd, 3rd, and 4th year of undergraduate program.
  - CGPA of 3.75 or above.
- **Benefits**:  
  - Tuition credit for a four-credit hour course in the subsequent term.
- **Combination**:  
  - In addition to Major and Minor Merit Scholarships and Need Based Scholarships.

## **3. GRADUATE SCHOLARSHIPS AT LAHORE SCHOOL**
### **3.1 Need Based Scholarship for Lahore School Graduates for MBA Programme**
- **Coverage**: 50% of total tuition fees for the term.
- **Eligibility Criteria**:
  1. CGPA of 3.5 in Lahore School’s 4-Year Bachelor Degree Program and Lahore School Need-Based Scholarship at the undergraduate level.
  2. Demonstrated need (Financial Assistance Form and home verification).
- **Maintenance Criteria**:  
  - CGPA of 3.5 required.
  - CGPA 3.49-3.0: 25% scholarship.
  - CGPA < 3.0: Not eligible.
- **Deadline**: July 15th of the relevant admission academic year.
- **Exclusivity**: Not in addition to Major Merit Scholarship.

### **3.2 Special Need Based Scholarship**
- **Eligibility**:  
  - Single-parent students (father deceased or parents divorced).
  - Students with disabilities meeting academic and financial criteria.
- **Benefits**:  
  - 25% additional scholarship for single-parent students.
  - Up to 100% tuition fees for students with disabilities.
- **Maintenance Criteria**:  
  - CGPA of 3.0 for single-parent students.
  - CGPA of 2.5 for students with disabilities.

### **3.3 Graduate Major Merit Scholarship for MBA Programme**
- **Eligibility**:  
  - BBA/BSc (Hons) graduates with CGPA of 3.75.
- **Benefits**:  
  - 50% Major Merit Scholarship (tuition fees for the first term).
- **Maintenance Criteria**:  
  - CGPA of 3.75: Full scholarship.
  - CGPA 3.5-3.74: 25% scholarship.
  - CGPA < 3.5: Scholarship withdrawn.

### **4. General Exclusions**
- **Major Merit Scholarship**: Not in addition to Need Based Scholarship.
- **Need Based Scholarship**: Not in addition to Major Merit Scholarship.
- **Special Scholarships**: May not be combined with other scholarships.
### **Structured Information from the Page**

## **1. Graduate Minor Merit Scholarship for MBA Programme**
- **Eligibility**: Students enrolled in the MBA programme with a CGPA of **3.75 and above** at the end of each regular term.
- **Benefit**: Fee waiver for a **three credit hour course** in the subsequent term.
- **Notes**: 
  - This scholarship is **in addition to the Major Merit Scholarship and Need Based Scholarships**.

## **2. Student TA Ships**
- **Eligibility**: 
  - Students of the Lahore School of Economics enrolled in the MBA Programme.
  - Not on **100% scholarship** and who attain a CGPA of **3.5 and above** at the end of their Hons. degree.
  - All **2nd year students** of the MBA programme with a CGPA of **3.5 and above** at the end of the **1st year**.
- **Benefit**: One Student TA Ship with tuition credit for a **three credit hour course** in the subsequent term.
- **Notes**: 
  - This scholarship is **in addition to the Major and Minor Merit Scholarships and Need Based Scholarships**.

## **3. Mahmood Chaudhry Scholarship**
- **Eligibility**: One MBA student enrolled in the **1½ year MBA Programme** who:
  - Is already on **Need Based Scholarship**.
  - Secures the **highest CGPA** at the end of the first term.
- **Benefit**: **100% scholarship** for the entire remaining year of the **1½ year MBA Programme**.
- **Notes**: 
  - In case of a tie, the **Financial Assistance Committee** will decide the ‘Mahmood Chaudhry Scholar’ based on ‘the greatest need’.

## **4. Graduate Major Merit Scholarship for Executive MBA Programme**
- **Eligibility**: 
  - BBA/BSc (Hons.) graduates of the Lahore School of Economics with a CGPA of **3.75** by the end of their Hons. degree.
  - Admitted to the Lahore School’s **Executive MBA Programme**.
- **Benefit**: **50% Major Merit Scholarship** (equivalent to **50% of the total tuition fees** for the term) for the first term.
- **Continuation Criteria**: 
  - Maintain a CGPA of **3.75** at the end of each term.
- **Scholarship Reduction**: 
  - CGPA between **3.74 and 3.5**: Reduced to **25%**.
  - CGPA below **3.5**: Scholarship will be **withdrawn**.

## **5. Graduate Minor Merit Scholarship for Executive MBA Programme**
- **Eligibility**: Students enrolled in the **Executive MBA Programme** with a CGPA of **3.75 and above** at the end of each regular term.
- **Benefit**: Fee waiver for a **three credit hour course** in the subsequent term.
- **Notes**: 
  - This scholarship is **in addition to the Major Merit Scholarship**.

## **POST-GRADUATE SCHOLARSHIPS AT LAHORE SCHOOL**

## **6. Major Merit Scholarship for PhD Programmes in Economics and Business Administration**
- **Eligibility**: Students who have attained a CGPA of **3.0** at the end of their MPhil/MS degree and are admitted to the Lahore School’s **PhD Programmes in Economics and Business Administration**.
- **Benefit**: **100% Merit Scholarship** on the total tuition fees for the first term of the PhD Programme.
- **Continuation Criteria**: 
  - Maintain a CGPA of **3.0** at the end of each term.
- **Notes**: 
  - If CGPA drops below **3.0**, the scholarship will be **withdrawn**.

## **7. Major Merit Scholarship for MS/MPhil Programmes in Economics and Business Administration**
- **Eligibility**: Students who have attained a CGPA of **3.0** by the end of their Hons. degree and are admitted to the Lahore School’s **MS/MPhil Programmes in Economics and Business Administration**.
- **Benefit**: **100% Merit Scholarship** on the total tuition fees for the first term of the MS/MPhil Programme.
- **Continuation Criteria**: 
  - Maintain a CGPA of **3.0** at the end of each term.
- **Notes**: 
  - If CGPA drops below **3.0**, the scholarship will be **withdrawn**.

### **Note**
1. For all Foreign/Overseas seats, a regular fee of $1,000/-US Dollar additional shall be charged each year (each semester for Faculty of Pharmacy).
2. All government taxes will be applicable including Withholding Income Tax per year from all those students whose annual fee / dues is above the limit.

5. These dues are subject to revision at any time in subsequent semesters.
6. Each student admitted on “Self Finance” basis shall pay Rs. one lac (100,000) per year in addition to the normal semester fee. However, all admission regulations including eligibility criteria etc are also applicable on “Self Finance” seats.

The image presents a document titled "Guidelines for Applicants: How to proceed for admission after display of Merit lists" from the University of Sargodha (UOS). The document is divided into two main sections: "Checking of Merit Lists" and "What's Next? [Departmental Visit]".

*   **Checking of Merit Lists**
    *   The admission portal of UOS displays two types of lists:
        *   **Master Merit list**: A list of all candidates applied in a particular program, allowing applicants to check their merit number.
        *   **Merit list**: A list of applicants according to the number of seats available in a particular program.
    *   Applicants can track their position through the merit list or master list if their name is not available in the current merit list.
    *   It is crucial to check names carefully from the merit list, especially for those who have applied to multiple degree programs, to avoid program transfer hassles.
    *   Congratulatory message for those whose names are in the merit list.

*   **What's Next? [Departmental Visit]**
    *   **Step 5: Departmental Visit**
        *   Applicants must visit the department with the following documents:
            *   All Academic Record (Original Certificate/Degree)
            *   Applicant's original CNIC/B-Form
            *   Original Test results (where applicable)
            *   Admission Fee (First semester fee)
    *   **Step 6: Visit Focal Person**
        *   Applicants need to visit the Focal Person of their degree program.
    *   **Step 7: Verification and Fee Voucher**
        *   The Focal Person will verify all documents. Upon successful verification, applicants will be given a Fee Voucher.

In summary, the document provides step-by-step guidelines for applicants to follow after the display of merit lists for admission to the University of Sargodha. It outlines the process of checking merit lists, understanding the different types of lists, and the necessary steps to take after being selected, including visiting the department with required documents and obtaining a Fee Voucher.

## **9. Social Responsibility**
### **9.1 Aim**
- **Aim**: To create conditions under which students of IMS, its staff, and the wider community are inspired and supported to engage with and contribute to social responsibility and sustainability across the University and beyond.

### **9.2 Objectives**
- **Objectives**:
  1. Minimize its environmental impact.
  2. Maximize its contribution to society.
  3. Have infrastructure that is developed and, where possible, operated to meet national and international environmental sustainability and social responsibility objectives.
  4. Exhibit high ethical standards.

### **9.3 Strategies**
- **Strategies**:
  1. Embedding its commitment to social responsibility and sustainability in its curricula, policies, and strategic procedures.
  2. Motivating all members of the Institute community to become effective advocates who actively support best practices, innovation, and leadership with regard to social responsibility and sustainability.
  3. Offering every student opportunities to study the broader aspects of current global challenges, social responsibility, and sustainability, and to explore in depth how their chosen subjects relate to them.
  4. Demonstrating and reporting its main social, environmental, and economic impacts.
  5. Recognizing and sharing its current expertise, opportunities, activities, and achievements relating to social responsibility, sustainability, and the global challenges.
  6. Improving its overall management of resources to reduce waste.

## **1. Program Overview**
- **Program Name**: Bachelor of Studies in Islamic Banking and Finance (BSIBF).
- **Duration**: Four-year undergraduate degree program.
- **Credit Hours**: 130 credit hours.
- **Focus**: Integrates modern banking and financial knowledge with Shariah-compliant finance.
- **Industry Context**: Addresses the growing need for skilled professionals in Islamic finance in Pakistan and globally.

## **2. Curriculum Structure**
### **Initial Semesters**
- **Foundational Subjects**:
  - English
  - Economics
  - Mathematics
  - Accounting
  - Management
  - Law
- **Islamic Studies**:
  - Islamic Studies
  - Tafseer (Quranic Exegesis)
  - Hadith (Prophetic Traditions)
  - Fiqh (Islamic Jurisprudence)

### **Advanced Semesters**
- **Specialized Courses**:
  - Islamic Banking Operations
  - Takaful (Islamic Insurance)
  - Islamic Capital Markets
  - Sukuk Structuring
  - Risk Management in Islamic Finance
  - Islamic Microfinance
  - Shariah Audit & Governance

## **3. Program Objectives (POs)**
The BSIBF program aims to achieve the following objectives:
1. **PO1**: Provide students with strong foundations in economics, banking, finance, accounting, and Shariah principles.
2. **PO2**: Develop expertise in Islamic banking operations, Islamic financial instruments, and risk management.
3. **PO3**: Equip students with skills in applying Shariah-compliant solutions to modern financial challenges.
4. **PO4**: Introduce students to the principles and applications of Islamic commercial law (Fiqh al-Muamalat).
5. **PO5**: Enhance analytical and problem-solving skills in structuring innovative Islamic financial products.
6. **PO6**: Promote research, innovation, and ethical responsibility in Islamic banking and finance practices.
7. **PO7**: Cultivate leadership, teamwork, and professional communication skills with a strong ethical foundation.
8. **PO8**: Prepare students for professional certifications (e.g., AAOIFI, IFQ, CIPA, CFA Islamic Finance) and entrepreneurial opportunities.

## **4. Learning Outcomes (LOs)**
By the end of the program, graduates will be able to:
1. **LO1**: Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of economics, finance, management, and Shariah principles applied to banking and finance.
2. **LO2**: Apply Islamic finance theories, tools, and techniques to solve real-world banking and financial problems.
3. **LO3**: Design, analyze, and evaluate Islamic financial products such as Mudarabah, Musharakah, Ijarah, Murabaha, and Sukuk.
4. **LO4**: Interpret and implement Shariah standards (AAOIFI, IFSB) in banking operations.

## **5. Practical Experience**
- **Internships**: Hands-on experience in Islamic banks and financial institutions.
- **Field Visits**: Visits to Islamic banks and financial institutions.
- **Workshops & Seminars**: Industry workshops and seminars.
- **Capstone Project/Thesis**: Final-year project where students solve real-world Islamic finance challenges.

## **6. Career Opportunities**
Graduates can work as:
- Islamic bankers
- Financial analysts
- Shariah compliance officers
- Investment advisors
- Consultants
- Researchers
- Entrepreneurs (Shariah-compliant ventures)

### **Major (Disciplinary) Requirements**

* A major is an academic discipline or specialized area of study.
* Minimum requirement: 72 credit hours for a single major, 84 credit hours for BSIBF, in line with NBEAC requirements.

### **Interdisciplinary/Allied Courses**

* All undergraduate programs must include a mandatory set of at least 12 credit hours.
* Exception: Associate Degrees require different credit hours.

### **Minor (Optional)**

* A minor is a secondary concentration of courses in an academic discipline.
* Requirements and prerequisites determined by the concerned department.
* Minimum 12 credit hours.

### **Admission Eligibility Criteria**

1. **Complete Course of BSIBF:** 130 credit hours.
2. **Applicants' Eligibility:** Minimum 45% in Higher Secondary School Certificate (F.Sc, FA) or equivalent from a recognized institute/board.

## **2. Learning Outcomes**
The workshops/seminars aim to:
1. Provide real-world exposure to **Islamic banking and financial services**.  
2. Bridge the gap between **academic concepts and industry practices**.  
3. Update students on **emerging trends in Islamic finance, fintech, and Shariah-compliant markets**.  
4. Enhance **networking opportunities** with practitioners, regulators, and Shariah scholars.  

## **3. Semester-Wise Examples of Workshop Themes**
### **Foundation (Semester 1-2)**
- **Introduction to Islamic Banking in Pakistan**  
- **Principles of Shariah-Compliant Finance**  

### **Intermediate (Semester 3-4)**
- **Islamic Modes of Financing (Murabaha, Ijarah, Mudarabah, Musharakah)**  
- **Professional Communication in Islamic Financial Institutions**  

### **Advanced Skills (Semester 5-6)**
- **Sukuk Structuring and Islamic Capital Markets**  
- **Risk Management and Takaful in Islamic Finance**  

### **Strategic & Professional (Semester 7-8)**
- **Fintech Innovations in Islamic Banking & Digital Finance**  
- **Launching Shariah-Compliant Startups & Microfinance Models**  

## **4. Exhibition**
- **One Islamic Banking & Finance Exhibition** will be organized annually.  
- **Objectives**:  
  - Students will showcase **Islamic financial models, Shariah-compliant products, sukuk structures, research projects, and fintech ideas**.  
  - **Industry experts, Shariah scholars, entrepreneurs, and alumni** will be invited to evaluate and mentor students.  
  - The exhibition will promote **creativity, innovation, and industry-academia linkages**.  

## **5. Faculty Coordinator**
- **Designation**: One faculty member will be officially notified as the **Workshops, Seminars, and Exhibition Coordinator for BSIBF**.  
- **Responsibilities**:  
  1. **Scheduling events**.  
  2. **Ensuring industry relevance**.  
  3. **Managing logistics**.  
  4. **Maintaining event records** (photos, videos, attendance, etc.).  
  5. **Ensuring media coverage** (press clippings, social media).  

## **6. Integration with Curriculum (Without Marks)**
- Students must prepare a **reflection note (1–2 pages)** after each workshop/seminar and submit it to the Coordinator for record-keeping.  
- Students will be awarded a **Certificate of Participation** (Rs.100 per certificate to cover expenses).  
- Certificates will be signed by the **Director, Centre for Islamic Banking & Finance, and the Coordinator**.

## SZABIST University

### Overview

* **Established:** 1995
* **Type:** Degree Awarding Institute (DAI)
* **Chartered as a University:** Sindh Act No. XIX of 2023
* **Rank:** One of the most reputed universities by the Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan and the Chartered Inspection and Evaluation Committee (CIEC)

### Vision

* SZABIST aims to be a globally recognized institute for excellence in education, research, development, and distinction in service.

### Mission

* SZABIST is committed to producing **highly qualified professionals** to:
  + Meet national and global contemporary needs
  + Conduct cutting-edge research and development
  + Provide hi-tech scientific and technological expertise
  + Meet current and future socio-economic challenges
  + Meet global citizenship responsibility

### Campuses

* SZABIST University comprises **six campuses** located in:
  + Karachi
  + Hyderabad
  + Larkana
  + Islamabad
  + Dubai (UAE)
  + Gharo
* **Current student population:** approximately **19,000**
* **Graduates:** over **31,000** talented graduates have been proudly awarded degrees by all campuses of SZABIST University

### Accreditation

* All programs of SZABIST University are conducted under strict compliance of the relevant regulatory bodies such as:
  + NBEAC
  + NCEAC
  + NACTE
  + PEC
  + KHDA Dubai (UAE)

### Faculty

* The university offers undergraduate, graduate, and post-graduate degrees in **5 different disciplines:**
  + Faculty of Management Sciences
  + Faculty of Computing and Engineering Sciences
  + Faculty of Media Sciences
  + Faculty of Education and Social Sciences
  + Faculty of Life Sciences
* Additionally, SZABIST University offers:
  + LLB program
  + CertHE (Law)
  + BABS program as external programs in affiliation with:
    - University of London
    - Coventry University, UK

### Partnerships

* SZABIST University has signed MoUs with various reputable and distinguished organizations and institutions such as:
  + Istanbul Medipol University, Turkey
  + University of London, UK
  + John Hopkins University (Gates Institute)
  + TCF
  + ICRC
  + Aman Foundation
  + JS Bank

### Department of Robotics and Artificial Intelligence

* The Department of Robotics and Artificial Intelligence at Shaheed Zulfikar Ali Bhutto Institute of Science and Technology (SZABIST) University stands at the forefront of nurturing innovation and excellence in the fields of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science (DS).
* **Mission:** equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary to transform complex scenarios into actionable decisions through in-depth understanding and application of AI principles.
* SZABIST University believes in conceptual learning and has developed a robust organizational network of reputable multinational and national enterprises for collaboration in computing. This network, along with its own software house, facilitates students in meeting the desired program educational objectives and learning outcomes, ensuring they are well-prepared for the challenges of the modern world.

## Master of Science in Data Science (MSDS)

### Program Overview

* **Program Name:** Master of Science in Data Science (MSDS)
* **Duration:** 2 years (evening program, 30 credit hours)
* **Curriculum:**
	+ 3 core courses
	+ 2 specialization courses in data science
	+ 3 elective courses
* **Completion Options:**
	+ Coursework
	+ Research work
* **Additional Requirements:**
	+ 2 additional courses (3 credit hours each) for coursework route
	+ 2 Independent Research Studies (IRS) or a research thesis (6 credit hours) for research path

### Admission Requirements

* **Education:** 16 years of relevant education (4 years BS(CS) or equivalent)
* **Minimum Marks:** 50% marks / 2.0 CGPA from HEC recognized University/Institute
* **Eligible Disciplines:**
	+ Information Technology
	+ Software Engineering
	+ Computer Engineering
	+ Electrical Engineering
	+ Statistics
	+ Mathematics
	+ Business Administration
	+ Accounting & Finance
	+ Economics
	+ other relevant disciplines
* **Deficiency Courses:**
	1. Programming Fundamentals (Core Programming Course)
	2. Data Structures & Algorithms OR Design & Analysis of Algorithms
	3. Database Systems
* **Test Requirements:**
	+ GAT (General) or HAT or relevant with a minimum 50% score
	+ Last Degree verification from Higher Education Commission (HEC) is required

### Fee Structure

* **Application Processing Fee:** Rs. 2,000/-
* **Admission Fee:** Rs. 25,000/-
* **Security Deposit (refundable):** Rs. 15,000/-
* **Student Activity Charges:** Rs. 1,500/-
* **Tuition Fee (Per Semester 3 Courses):** Rs. 73,800/-
* **Course Registration Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-
* **Examination Fee Per Course:** Rs. 500/-
* **Final Open Defense for MS by Research Program charges:** Rs. 10,000/-

### Admission Schedule

* **Admissions Start:** May 27, 2024
* **Last date to Apply:** July 24, 2024
* **Admission Test:** July 29-31, 2024
* **Interviews:** August 06 to 09, 2024
* **Classes Commence:** September 09, 2024

- **Credit Hours Required**: Students must complete **132 Credit Hours** to be awarded the degree.

## **3.3 Assessment Criteria of Subjects/Courses**
- **Mid-Term Evaluation**: **20%** (Mid-Term Examination)
- **Final Examination**: **60%** (Final Term Examination)
- **Quizzes, Assignments, and Presentations**: **20%** (distributed as: 
  - **Quizzes**: **5%** 
  - **Assignments**: **5%** 
  - **Presentations**: **10%**)

## **3.4 Duration of BS Statistics**
- **Minimum Duration**: **4 Years** or **8 Semesters**
- **Maximum Duration**: **6 Years** or **12 Semesters**

### **Degree Awarded**:
- **Associate Degree in Statistics**

- **2-3 Years** ( **4-6 Semesters** )

- **16-18 weeks**

### **Total Credit Hours**:
- **60-72**

## Meeting Details

* **Meeting Name:** Minutes of the Meeting of the Committee for Preparation of Proposal for Teaching of Holy Quran
* **Date:** 17 April, 2023
* **Location:** Committee room, University of Swat

## Committee Members

1. Prof. Dr. Fazal Mabood (Convener) - Dean, Faculty of Management and Social Sciences
2. Dr. Mushtaq Ahmed - Associate Professor, Chairman, Department of Islamic & Arabic Studies
3. Dr. Lutfullah - Chairman Department of Law and Sharia
4. Dr. Umar Hayat - Chairman Department of Economics and Development Studies
5. Registrar, University of Swat
6. Dr. Yahya Khan (Secretary/Member)

## Meeting Summary

* The meeting started with the recitation from Holy Quran
* The Chairman welcomed all participants
* Dr. Yahya Khan presented a brief about the decision of the Senate of Pakistan and subsequent letter of higher education commission of Pakistan to universities regarding the same
* He also briefed the committee about practice of other universities

## Syllabus Discussion

* The syllabus of University of Malakand is presented before the committee, which is already approved from relevant bodies and currently implemented by the University of Malakand
* The teaching of The Holy Quran in said Syllabus is divided into eight semesters and 02 credit hours is allocated in each semester to cover the translation of the Holy Quran in the Degree Program

## Proposal

* The proposal from the Department of Islamic and Arabic Studies, University of Swat was also presented which is also in line with the pattern of the University of Malakand

## Committee Decisions

1. The proposal/syllabus presented regarding the teaching of holy Quran in each Degree program is hereby recommended in line with the already approved and implemented syllabus of university of Malakand (copy attached)
2. Due care may be taken while assigning the subject of teaching of the Holy Quran to a teacher
3. *The Course shall be non-credit Compulsory part of each semester & it will have 100 marks. Passing marks will be 50 but in transcript only *passed/fail (P/F) shall be written.*

## Additional Information

* Meeting ended with vote of thanks to and the chair.
